items.title: Andy Jassy Just Said Something Big: Here's What It Means for Amazon's Stock
items.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy called generative AI a 'once-in-a-generation opportunity' to justify the company's massive $200 billion capital expenditure forecast for 2026. While Jassy compares current AI investments to AWS's successful rollout in the 2000s, the article cautions that such heavy spending on data centers carries risk if anticipated demand doesn't materialize. Investors should await more tangible progress before getting excited about the strategy.

items.title: OpenAI's IPO Dream Gets Massive Boost As Microsoft Reportedly Agrees To $38 Billion Revenue Cap— Nadella Says 'Very Proud' Of Early Bet
items.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI have renegotiated their partnership terms, capping total revenue-sharing payments at $38 billion. The deal allows OpenAI greater flexibility to work with other cloud providers like Amazon and Google while Microsoft remains the primary partner. Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella expressed pride in the company's early investment in OpenAI. These terms could strengthen OpenAI's appeal to investors ahead of a potential IPO by year-end.

items.title: ROSEN, A GLOBAL AND LEADING LAW FIRM, Encourages Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - BW
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. (BW) for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation contract with Applied Digital. The lawsuit claims B&W's largest shareholder BRC had undisclosed conflicts of interest and that Applied Digital may not have actually needed the products/services, raising questions about revenue recognition. Investors who purchased B&W securities between November 5, 2025 and March 11, 2026 may be eligible for compensation, with a June 15, 2026 deadline to serve as lead plaintiff.

items.title: BABCOCK & WILCOX CLASS ACTION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Urges Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Investors with Large Losses to Contact the Firm
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. (BW) for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation Contract with Applied Digital. The lawsuit claims that B&W's largest shareholder BRC Group Holdings had undisclosed conflicts of interest and that Applied Digital may not have actually needed the products/services, raising questions about revenue recognition and overstated business prospects.

items.title: What Happens to Taiwan Semiconductor Stock if the AI Build-Out Slows Down? Here's My Answer.
items.summary: TSMC's stock has surged nearly 150% over the past year, driven by robust AI demand from hyperscalers. While Q1 2026 results showed strong 40.6% revenue growth and management raised full-year guidance, the stock's rich 35x P/E valuation leaves little room for error. The author warns that if AI infrastructure spending from a handful of major U.S. hyperscalers slows, TSMC could face significant headwinds, rating the stock a 'hold' rather than a 'buy' given the risks.

items.title: After an 11% Drop, Is CoreWeave a Buy on the Dip or a Stock to Avoid?
items.summary: CoreWeave, a specialized AI compute provider, has dropped 11% following earnings despite doubling revenue to $2 billion. While the company boasts a $100 billion backlog and strong demand for GPU services, investor concerns about heavy debt reliance and increased capital spending forecasts ($31 billion) have pressured the stock. The article suggests CoreWeave could be attractive for aggressive investors betting on AI growth, but cautious investors may prefer established cloud leaders.

items.title: This AI Stock Is Crushing Nvidia in 2026. It's Still a Buy After Soaring 240% This Year, According to Wall Street.
items.summary: DigitalOcean has surged 240% in 2026 by pivoting to AI services, significantly outperforming Nvidia's 15% gain. While Nvidia maintains dominance in AI infrastructure with 90% market share in accelerators and strong competitive moats, DigitalOcean's AI-Native Cloud platform targets the growing inference market. However, at 81x earnings, DigitalOcean appears expensive despite strong guidance, while Nvidia at 45x earnings with 53% annual earnings growth through 2028 offers better value for patient investors.

items.title: 3 Top Stocks to Buy in May
items.summary: The article recommends three stocks for May 2026: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), Amazon (AMZN), and Lemonade (LMND). TSMC benefits from strong AI chip demand with 41% YoY revenue growth and 66.2% gross margin. Amazon shows accelerated growth across AWS, e-commerce, and advertising, with AI driving triple-digit revenue growth. Lemonade, an AI-powered insurance disruptor, achieved 32% YoY IFP growth and is expected to reach profitability by year-end.

items.title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock: Alphabet or Microsoft
items.summary: The article compares Alphabet and Microsoft as AI hyperscalers investing heavily in cloud computing infrastructure. While Alphabet demonstrates stronger growth metrics (22% revenue growth vs. 18% for Microsoft) and advantages in cloud computing with its TPU chips, Microsoft emerges as the better investment due to significantly cheaper valuation. Alphabet trades at decade-high levels while Microsoft is near decade lows, making Microsoft the more attractive option despite Alphabet appearing as the healthier company.

items.title: Prediction: AI Infrastructure Stocks Will Crush the S&P 500 in 2026
items.summary: Tech giants are investing nearly $700 billion in AI infrastructure build-out in 2026 to meet current customer demand. As AI moves from research into real-world applications, infrastructure companies providing chips, servers, and networking equipment are positioned for explosive growth. With attractive valuations at seven-year lows and strong revenue momentum, AI infrastructure stocks are predicted to outperform the broader S&P 500.

items.title: 3 Growth Stocks Down 20% to Buy Right Now
items.summary: Despite record-high stock indexes, three growth stocks trading significantly below their all-time highs present potential buying opportunities. MercadoLibre faces margin pressures and loan defaults but maintains strong 49% revenue growth. Chewy has recovered operationally with 125% operating income surge despite being down 80% from peaks. Shopify's rapid 34% revenue growth and 88% operating income increase are overshadowed by AI-related concerns, though its ecosystem moat remains defensible.

items.title: Uber's $10 Billion Robotaxi Push Could Challenge Tesla And Waymo Dominance
items.summary: Uber is committing $10 billion to its robotaxi ambitions, allocating $2.5 billion for equity investments in robotaxi operators and $7.5 billion for fleet expansion starting in 2027, targeting over 100,000 vehicles. The company is partnering with multiple manufacturers including Lucid, Nuro, and others, leveraging NVIDIA technology. CEO Dara Khosrowshahi views the autonomous vehicle sector as a trillion-dollar market opportunity. Investor Gary Black predicts Uber could lead the global robotaxi sector over Tesla and Waymo due to its operational leverage and massive user base of 200 million monthly active customers.

items.title: A wealth advisor said goodbye to a tech ETF but not to the sector
items.summary: Resolute Wealth Strategies liquidated its entire position in JPMorgan U.S. Tech Leaders ETF (JTEK), selling 34,133 shares worth approximately $2.93 million in May 2026. However, the firm maintained its overall technology sector exposure through individual large-cap tech stocks and other sector-tilted ETFs, suggesting this was a strategic portfolio restructuring rather than a bearish tech call.

items.title: Is CoreWeave Stock a Buy on the Dip as Revenue Continues to Skyrocket?
items.summary: CoreWeave's stock fell despite reporting 112% revenue growth to $2.08 billion and a nearly $100 billion project backlog. However, the company's adjusted EPS loss widened to $1.12 (worse than expected $0.91), Q2 guidance missed consensus, and rising component costs forced the company to increase its capex budget. CoreWeave remains highly leveraged and dependent on off-the-shelf GPUs, making it vulnerable to rising chip prices.

items.title: Why Caterpillar Could Be the AI Stock of the Year
items.summary: Caterpillar, a century-old construction equipment manufacturer, is emerging as a compelling AI infrastructure play due to massive demand for data center construction from tech giants like Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft. The company reported a 22% revenue increase to $17.4 billion in Q1 2026, record backlogs, and a 170% stock surge over 12 months, positioning it as an overlooked but critical player in the AI physical infrastructure build-out.

items.title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy Has Good News and Bad News for Nvidia Investors
items.summary: Amazon is increasing AI chip purchases but shifting focus toward its own custom Trainium accelerators instead of Nvidia GPUs. CEO Andy Jassy revealed that AWS is deploying more Trainium chips than Nvidia chips and has a $225 billion backlog for Trainium compute. Other cloud providers like Google and Microsoft are similarly developing custom chips, threatening Nvidia's dominant market position in AI accelerators.

items.title: Jensen Huang Just Made a Major Announcement. Here's What It Means for Nvidia Investors.
items.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang announced the creation of Nvidia Ising, the world's first family of open-source quantum AI models. Rather than building quantum computing hardware like competitors, Nvidia is positioning itself to control the software 'brains' of quantum systems. While quantum computing remains years away from commercial viability, this move demonstrates Nvidia's long-term strategic vision beyond its current AI GPU dominance.

items.title: Has Amazon Found the Next AWS?
items.summary: Amazon has launched Amazon Supply Chain Services (ASCS), opening its logistics network to external businesses similar to how AWS operates. The service, already adopted by major corporations like Procter & Gamble and 3M, could become a high-margin profit driver. While AWS remains Amazon's strongest business segment, ASCS represents another growth avenue, though its long-term success remains uncertain.

items.title: Missed April's AI Rally? These 3 Stocks Still Look Like Bargains.
items.summary: Despite April's AI sector rally, three megacap tech stocks remain attractive bargains. Microsoft trades 22% below its October high despite strong 18% revenue growth and 40% Azure growth. Nvidia, though near all-time highs, could see 40% upside as companies increase 2027 capital expenditures. Meta offers undervalued AI exposure with 33% revenue growth, trading below market multiples while growing faster than average.

items.title: Prediction: Amazon Will Outperform the S&P 500
items.summary: Amazon is positioned to outperform the S&P 500 in 2026 due to accelerating AWS cloud revenue growth (28% in Q1), expanding chip business ($20B run-rate), strong positioning in AI infrastructure, and underappreciated e-commerce efficiency gains through robotics and AI. The stock trades at an attractive 32x forward P/E compared to peers, offering room for growth and multiple expansion.

items.title: 3 Lessons for Transportation Industry Investors Following Spirit Airlines' Bankruptcy
items.summary: Spirit Airlines' bankruptcy provides three key lessons for transportation investors: avoid buying stocks immediately after bankruptcy emergence as companies often face recurring financial troubles, be cautious when struggling companies attempt major acquisitions as a last resort, and carefully monitor airline balance sheets and leverage ratios since high debt levels leave little room to weather adversity like fuel price spikes.

items.title: 2 Stocks With Monster Potential to Hold Through the Next Decade of Chaos
items.summary: The article recommends Cameco and Alphabet as two stocks well-positioned to thrive amid global chaos and market volatility. Cameco, the world's second-largest uranium miner, benefits from accelerating nuclear energy demand with 75 reactors under construction and 120 planned. The company reported strong Q1 2026 results with 7% revenue growth and 88% EPS surge. Alphabet, positioned on both hardware and software sides of AI, grew Q1 2026 revenue 22% to $109.8B and EPS 82% to $5.11, with expanding profit margins.

items.title: Want SpaceX Stock Before the IPO? These 3 Plays Give You a Back Door In.
items.summary: SpaceX is preparing for an IPO expected in early June 2026, with a potential valuation of $2 trillion. Three publicly traded companies offer indirect exposure to SpaceX: Alphabet (6% stake), Bank of America (less than 1% stake), and EchoStar (potential 2.8% stake pending regulatory approval). EchoStar's potential SpaceX position could be worth around $56 billion if the deal is approved.

items.title: Shell's CEO Says the Oil Market Is Short 1 Billion Barrels and Getting Worse. Here's What Investors Should Do Now.
items.summary: Shell's CEO reports a global oil supply shortage of nearly 1 billion barrels due to the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure, with Persian Gulf production down 57%. The world is burning through stockpiles at record rates, and even with a peace deal, recovery could take months. Oil prices are expected to remain elevated through 2027, benefiting oil stocks while pressuring energy-intensive industries.

items.title: Amazon vs. Microsoft: Which Tech Giant Looks Better After Its Earnings Report?
items.summary: Amazon and Microsoft reported earnings on April 29, with diverging trajectories. Amazon's AWS grew 28% year-over-year with accelerating momentum, while Microsoft Cloud grew 29% but at a slower acceleration rate. Amazon achieved its highest net profit margin ever at 16.7%, driven by high-margin businesses like advertising and AI chips. Despite Microsoft's higher 38.3% net profit margin, Amazon's improving profitability and multiple growth avenues position it more favorably for long-term growth.

items.title: My Top 2 AI Stocks Coming off a Brutal Sell-Off for May 2026
items.summary: Following a market sell-off driven by geopolitical tensions and economic uncertainty, two AI stocks present buying opportunities: Snowflake, a cloud data warehouse platform serving 13,000+ customers with strong long-term growth prospects despite slowing revenue growth, and SoundHound AI, which is expanding into restaurant digitization and conversational AI markets through strategic acquisitions despite near-term margin pressures.

items.title: Arm Just Made a Bold Prediction, And It Could Be a Huge Buy Signal
items.summary: Arm Holdings forecasts becoming the CPU market leader by decade's end, with CEO Rene Haas predicting it will have the largest market share by CPU type. The company's new AGI CPU has demand exceeding $2 billion, more than double initial expectations. While supply chain constraints limit near-term fulfillment, Arm projects $15 billion in CPU revenue by fiscal 2031 and $25 billion in total revenue, with potential to significantly exceed these conservative forecasts.

items.title: Deal Dispatch: J.M. Smucker Considers Strategic Review, Meta Buys Assured Robot Intelligence, Buzzfeed On Brink Of Bankruptcy
items.summary: Multiple companies announced strategic reviews and M&A activity. J.M. Smucker hired Goldman Sachs for a portfolio review amid activist pressure, potentially leading to a Hostess sale. Meta acquired Assured Robot Intelligence for AI robotics development. Meanwhile, BuzzFeed faces bankruptcy risk after missing a $5 million debt payment with a May 18 deadline. Other notable deals include GameStop's $55 billion non-binding proposal for eBay, Angelini Pharma's $4.1 billion acquisition of Catalyst Pharmaceuticals, and Ametek's $5 billion purchase of Indicor's instrumentation businesses.

items.title: Computer Vision in Healthcare Market Report 2026: Revenue Expected to Surpass $33.4 Billion by 2036
items.summary: The global computer vision in healthcare market is projected to grow from $4.37 billion in 2026 to $33.4 billion by 2036, at a CAGR of 22.6%. Growth is driven by AI-integrated diagnostic systems, radiologist shortages, and precision medicine adoption. Software and medical imaging segments lead the market, while Asia-Pacific is expected to see the fastest regional growth.

items.title: Is Backblaze the Next Momentum Monster?
items.summary: Backblaze (BLZE) surged 64% following Q1 earnings, driven by its strategic positioning in the AI infrastructure market as a low-cost data lake provider for the emerging Neocloud ecosystem. The company is gaining traction with a 76% YOY increase in AI customers, expanding enterprise sales under new CRO leadership, and demonstrating improving unit economics with 26% adjusted EBITDA margins. Management projects positive adjusted free cash flow in H2 2026, signaling a transition to sustainable profitability.

items.title: Nvidia Stock Has Huge Momentum. Time to Buy?
items.summary: Nvidia stock has surged 19% over the last 30 days, driven by exceptional Q4 2026 results showing 73% revenue growth and record data center revenue of $62.3 billion. Management guidance projects 77% year-over-year growth for Q1 2026. While the P/E ratio of 43 appears reasonable relative to growth, significant risks exist including competition from customers developing their own chips, China market uncertainty, and questions about sustainability of hyperscaler spending.

items.title: Questex’s StreamTV Show 2026 Unveils Its Most Powerful Market Floor Yet - Fueled by Industry Giants with Expansive, Immersive Market Floor Experiences
items.summary: StreamTV Show 2026, taking place June 16-19 in Denver, will feature the largest market floor to date with major tech and advertising companies including Google, LG, and Vizio showcasing immersive brand experiences. Cineverse will anchor the event with a 40x40 booth highlighting its newly acquired companies and integrated streaming ecosystem through its Matchpoint platform.

items.title: Coinbase Valuation Depends on Lower Costs as Trading Volumes Fall
items.summary: Coinbase announced a 14% workforce reduction (700 positions) on May 5, 2026, citing AI automation as justification. The layoffs come amid a 50% Bitcoin decline from October 2025 peak ($125K to $62K) and 30% year-over-year drop in trading volumes. The restructuring targets $120M-$150M in annual operating expense savings against $50M-$60M one-time charges. Q1 2026 revenue fell 5% YoY to $1.6B despite the company holding $7.5B in cash.

items.title: Here's Why UPS Stock Is Rising and Falling in 2026
items.summary: UPS stock remains flat in 2026 despite April gains, facing headwinds from its voluntary 50% reduction in Amazon deliveries through mid-2026. While the company expects margin recovery in the second half, near-term profitability is pressured by network adjustment costs and broader package volume declines. Amazon's new logistics service (ASCS) poses an additional competitive threat to UPS's business.

items.title: Amazon, Google, Microsoft In Focus As EU Reportedly Mulls Restricting US Cloud Providers' Access To Sensitive Data
items.summary: The European Union is considering implementing regulations to restrict US cloud providers' access to sensitive government data as part of its upcoming 'Tech Sovereignty Package' to be presented on May 27. The proposals would limit but not fully exclude foreign cloud providers from handling classified public-sector information, affecting major US tech companies. This move is driven by concerns over the US Cloud Act and strained US-EU relations under the Trump administration.

items.title: Alphabet Can Become the Most Valuable Company Thanks to Google Cloud
items.summary: Alphabet is positioned to become the world's most valuable company, potentially surpassing Nvidia this year, driven by Google Cloud's impressive 63% year-over-year revenue growth and 81% net income increase in Q1. The company is demonstrating superior AI return on investment compared to competitors, with stronger profit margins and $126.8 billion in cash reserves enabling accelerated capital expenditures and market share gains. Emerging segments like Waymo and Gemini show additional long-term growth potential.

items.title: Amazon Just Announced Fantastic News for Investors: Should You Buy?
items.summary: Amazon launched Amazon Supply Chain Services, allowing third-party retailers to use its delivery and logistics network. The expansion of this existing infrastructure is expected to drive revenue growth and margin expansion. With higher-margin segments like advertising (22% YoY growth) and AWS (28% YoY growth, 35% operating margin) growing faster than the core business, Amazon's operating margin could expand from 12% to ~20% over the next few years. At a market cap of $2.9 trillion and trading at 16x forward earnings, the stock is fairly valued for a megacap with strong growth prospects.

items.title: 2 Outstanding AI Stocks to Buy and Hold for the Next Decade
items.summary: The article recommends Microsoft and Meta Platforms as two AI stocks worth buying and holding for the next decade. Despite recent stock price declines and investor concerns about heavy AI spending, both companies are showing strong financial performance with robust revenue growth driven by AI initiatives. Microsoft's Azure posted 40% year-over-year growth and its AI business has a $37 billion annual revenue run rate, while Meta's revenue grew 33% year-over-year. Both companies are positioned to deliver excellent long-term returns despite near-term market skepticism.

items.title: AI Stocks Were Just Put to the Test. Here's How They Fared.
items.summary: AI stocks faced significant headwinds in early 2026 due to valuation concerns, with the S&P 500 Shiller CAPE ratio reaching 40 (matching dot-com bubble levels), and geopolitical uncertainty from the Iran war. However, AI stocks have rebounded strongly as tech giants demonstrated that their massive AI infrastructure spending is justified by solid customer commitments and strong revenue growth. Companies like Amazon are monetizing their $200 billion AI investments through committed customer deals, proving the long-term viability of the AI investment thesis.

items.title: Vertiv: The Backlog is Massive, the Margin of Safety is Not
items.summary: Vertiv (VRT), a leader in data center power and thermal infrastructure for AI, has surged 300% in 12 months with a massive $15 billion backlog and improving unit economics. However, the stock trades at a 50.9x forward P/E multiple with only a 1.9% FCF yield, leaving minimal margin of safety despite strong fundamentals and growth prospects. The valuation assumes sustained high growth and successful services transformation, with execution risks from project delays and financing constraints.

items.title: This "Magnificent Seven" Stock Is the Cheapest of Them All. Is It a Buy Right Now?
items.summary: Meta Platforms is trading at the lowest valuation among the Magnificent Seven stocks at 19.8x forward P/E ratio. Despite a 6% decline this year, the article argues the stock is undervalued given strong Q1 advertising revenue growth (33% YoY) and strategic AI investments. Investor skepticism centers on Meta's increased capital expenditure plans ($125-145 billion), though the author contends this spending is necessary to remain competitive in AI infrastructure.

items.title: Did Amazon Just Give This Logisitcs Stock a No-Brainer Buying Opportunity?
items.summary: GXO Logistics stock plunged 18% after Amazon announced its new supply chain services business, but CEO Patrick Kelleher dismissed the threat, arguing that GXO's customized, specialized logistics solutions serve a different market than Amazon's pre-existing infrastructure offering. GXO reported strong Q1 earnings with 10.8% revenue growth and raised full-year guidance, with the CEO believing the stock sell-off presents a buying opportunity.

items.title: Meta's Earnings Got a Major Tax Boost. Here Are the Adjusted Figures You Need to See.
items.summary: Meta reported strong Q1 2026 revenue growth of 33% year-over-year, but an $8.03 billion tax benefit inflated reported earnings. Excluding this benefit, adjusted EPS growth was only 14%, significantly trailing revenue growth. The company raised 2026 capital expenditure guidance to $125-145 billion (nearly double 2025 spending), driven by higher component costs and data center investments. While the ad business accelerates and valuation appears reasonable at 20.5x forward P/E, the massive spending increase presents significant risk.

items.title: Why Amazon Might Be the Real Winner of the Microsoft and OpenAI Partnership Shakeup
items.summary: Microsoft's exclusive license to OpenAI's models has ended, allowing Amazon Web Services (AWS) to now offer OpenAI models to its customers. Combined with Amazon's $50 billion investment in OpenAI announced two months prior, this restructuring positions AWS to strengthen its competitive advantage in the cloud market. AWS, which generates 59% of Amazon's operating income despite only 20% of revenue, could see accelerated growth as it offers customers more AI model options.

items.title: Top AST SpaceMobile Insider Cuts Position by Over 30%
items.summary: Rakuten CEO Hiroshi Mikitani has aggressively reduced AST SpaceMobile holdings by approximately 32%, selling $271 million worth of shares in Q2 2026. While still holding over 21 million shares, Rakuten's position has fallen below the 10% threshold. Meanwhile, Marvell Technology sees increased insider selling amid strong stock performance, though most sales are pre-planned. e.l.f. Beauty insider sales appear procedural and non-concerning.

items.title: ChronoScale Appoints Cenly Chen as Chief Executive Officer to Lead Next Phase of AI Compute Growth
items.summary: ChronoScale Corporation announced the appointment of Cenly Chen as Chief Executive Officer, bringing over two decades of enterprise infrastructure and high-performance computing experience from Super Micro Computer. The appointment comes as demand for AI compute infrastructure accelerates, with ChronoScale positioning itself to expand capacity and deliver consistent performance at scale.

items.title: Thinking of Buying Pinterest? Stop and Do This Instead.
items.summary: Despite beating Q1 earnings expectations, Pinterest's growth is underwhelming compared to industry peers like Meta. The company remains unprofitable on a GAAP basis with high stock-based compensation (23% of revenue), and faces headwinds from AI-generated content. Meta significantly outperformed with 33% revenue growth and superior profit margins, making it a better investment choice.

items.title: ROSEN, GLOBAL INVESTOR COUNSEL, Encourages Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action - BW
items.summary: A securities class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises (BW) for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation Contract. The lawsuit claims that B&W's largest shareholder BRC Group Holdings stood on both sides of the contract and that Applied Digital did not actually need the products/services, raising questions about revenue recognition. Investors who purchased BW securities between November 5, 2025 and March 11, 2026 may be entitled to compensation, with a June 15, 2026 deadline to serve as lead plaintiff.

items.title: Alphabet Stock Gains On Report Of Massive Anthropic Cloud, Chip Deal
items.summary: Alphabet stock rose 1.35-1.40% to a new 52-week high after a report revealed Anthropic committed to spend $200 billion with Google Cloud over five years. The deal reportedly accounts for over 40% of Google's disclosed revenue backlog and validates Alphabet's cloud and AI infrastructure strategy, potentially easing concerns about Google Cloud's competitive position against Amazon and Microsoft.

items.title: Danaher Announces Quarterly Dividend
items.summary: Danaher Corporation announced that its Board of Directors has approved a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.40 per share, payable on July 31, 2026 to shareholders of record on June 26, 2026.

items.title: Apple, Amazon, TSMC Top List Of Earnings Triple Plays As Beat-And-Raise Stocks Surge
items.summary: Apple, Amazon, and TSMC are among the largest companies delivering earnings 'triple plays' in Q1 2026—beating EPS and revenue estimates while raising forward guidance. According to Bespoke Investment Group, 66 triple plays have occurred since mid-April, more than double last year's 30. Triple-play stocks averaged an 8.6% one-day gain after reporting, significantly above the five-year average of 5%, with 24 stocks gaining 10% or more. The trend spans multiple sectors including technology, healthcare, industrials, and consumer stocks.

items.title: Oracle Stock Is Still Down Year to Date, As Many AI Stocks Soar. Here's What's Going On.
items.summary: Oracle's stock has underperformed other AI stocks despite strong quarterly results and a massive $553 billion contracted backlog, largely driven by a $300 billion OpenAI deal. The company is taking on significant debt to fund AI infrastructure buildout, and investor concerns about OpenAI's ability to meet revenue targets and fund future commitments have weighed on the stock. Unlike competitors funding buildouts from internal cash flow, Oracle is leveraging debt to lock in long-dated compute contracts with uncertain economics.

items.title: 1 Update Amazon CEO Andy Jassy Just Said That Every Artificial Intelligence (AI) Investor Should Hear
items.summary: Amazon reported strong Q1 results with 17% sales growth and 30% operating income increase. AWS accelerated growth to 28% YoY, driven by AI demand. CEO Jassy emphasized the company's $200 billion 2026 capex investment in infrastructure (land, power, buildings, chips, servers) is essential for AI expansion, typically monetized 6-24 months after spending. The article highlights investment opportunities in AI supply chain companies providing critical components to hyperscalers.

items.title: 3 Next Generation Nuclear Energy Stocks Investors Should Be Watching Closely
items.summary: As global energy demand surges, three nuclear energy companies are positioned to lead the next-generation reactor market. Oklo develops advanced microreactors with strong customer demand but faces multi-year delays before operations. NuScale holds regulatory approval as the only certified SMR developer but needs to convert partnerships into binding deals. GE Vernova, an established power company, is actively constructing its first commercial SMR in Canada with near-term revenue from gas turbines funding development.

items.title: Tesla, Meta, Microsoft Investors May Need A 'Self-Destruct' Switch, Not Blind Faith
items.summary: An independent market commentator argues that investors in major AI stocks like Tesla, Meta, and Microsoft should establish predetermined conditions to abandon their investment thesis when evidence turns against it. The article warns that powerful AI narratives have hardened into doctrine, with valuations heavily dependent on distant future assumptions. It emphasizes that massive capital expenditures don't automatically create competitive moats and that investors need clear metrics—such as deployment milestones, revenue conversion, and unit economics—to validate their convictions before belief overtakes analysis.

items.title: 1 Unstoppable Stock to Buy Before It Joins Nvidia, Alphabet, Microsoft, and Apple in the $3 Trillion Club
items.summary: Meta Platforms is leveraging artificial intelligence to transform Facebook and Instagram into personalized content hubs, driving increased user engagement and advertising revenue. With a market cap of $1.55 trillion and trading at a P/E ratio of 22.1 (below the Nasdaq-100 average of 33.9), Meta could potentially double in value to reach $3 trillion by next year. The company's Q1 2026 revenue grew 33% year-over-year to $56.3 billion, though significant capital expenditures of up to $145 billion in 2026 for AI infrastructure will impact near-term earnings.

items.title: Prediction: The Best Artificial Intelligence (AI) Growth Stocks on the Nasdaq Haven't Peaked Yet. Here's What I'm Buying.
items.summary: The article argues that AI growth stocks focused on infrastructure rather than headline names offer compelling opportunities. Credo Technology Group dominates the Active Electrical Cables market with 202% YoY revenue growth, while UiPath has repositioned itself as an enterprise agentic AI execution layer and achieved GAAP profitability for the first time. Both companies benefit from hyperscaler AI infrastructure spending but face execution risks from customer concentration and competitive pressure from larger vendors.

items.title: Meta and Alphabet Will Spend a Combined $335 Billion This Year. Don't Expect Their ROI to Be the Same.
items.summary: Alphabet and Meta are increasing AI capital expenditures to $335 billion combined in 2026, with Alphabet spending $190 billion and Meta $145 billion. However, investors are responding differently to their strategies: Alphabet has a clear AI monetization plan with surging Google Cloud revenue (up 63%) and growing Gemini Enterprise users, while Meta lacks a precise plan for ROI despite doubling its capex spending. Meta's stock fell 10% while Alphabet's rose over 10% following the announcements.

items.title: Roundhill Files for 'Magnificent Seven Plus' ETF With AI, Space Twist
items.summary: Roundhill Investments has filed to launch the Roundhill Magnificent Seven Plus ETF (ticker: MAGP), which would combine exposure to the dominant mega-cap tech stocks (the 'Magnificent Seven') with newer AI and space companies including Broadcom, Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX. The fund remains subject to regulatory approval, with expense ratio and launch date to be announced.

items.title: BW Investor Alert: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises Securities Fraud Lawsuit - Investors With Losses May Seek to Lead the Class Action After Company Allegedly Fabricated Contract Value: Levi & Korsinsky
items.summary: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises is facing a securities fraud lawsuit alleging the company misrepresented a $2.4 billion power generation contract with Applied Digital. The contract contained only $434 million in fixed fees with $1.96 billion unguaranteed, and Applied Digital could exit its guarantee for as little as $50 million. The stock fell 11.59% on March 12, 2026, when a short seller report exposed the discrepancies. The lead plaintiff deadline is June 15, 2026.

items.title: Meta's Revenue Growth Is Impressive, but Its AI Budget Is Getting Harder to Ignore
items.summary: Meta reported impressive 33% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026, but the stock declined following the announcement due to escalating AI infrastructure spending. The company raised its full-year 2026 capital expenditure guidance to $125-145 billion (nearly double 2025's $72 billion), citing higher component and data center costs. While management emphasizes efficiency improvements through custom silicon and AMD chips, investors remain concerned about whether these massive investments will generate adequate returns.

items.title: BABCOCK & WILCOX CLASS ACTION ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Urges Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Investors with Large Losses to Contact the Firm
items.summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. (BW) for allegedly making false and misleading statements regarding a Power Generation Contract with Applied Digital. The lawsuit claims that B&W's largest shareholder BRC Group Holdings stood on both sides of the deal and that Applied Digital did not actually need the products/services, raising questions about revenue recognition and overstating business prospects. Investors who purchased BW securities between November 5, 2025 and March 11, 2026 can join the lawsuit with a deadline of June 15, 2026 to apply as lead plaintiff.

items.title: Big-Tech Earnings: Google and Meta’s Results Support Broadcom’s Outlook
items.summary: Google and Meta reported strong earnings with significant AI capital expenditure increases, benefiting their semiconductor partner Broadcom. Google's cloud business grew 63% with a nearly doubled backlog, while Meta's advertising business accelerated. Both companies signaled continued or increased spending on AI infrastructure in 2027, supporting demand for Broadcom's custom chips.

items.title: Broadcom Valuation Tests Limits as AI Revenue Targets Stretch Expectations
items.summary: Broadcom faces a critical test between aggressive AI revenue growth targets and valuation concerns. CEO Hock Tan projects $100 billion in AI chip revenue for 2027 (2.7x total 2025 revenue), with Q2 guidance of $22 billion implying 46.6% YoY growth and $10.7 billion in AI revenue. The company has secured supply chain commitments and diversified customer base including Google, Meta, OpenAI, and Anthropic. However, the stock's P/E of 80.60 and proximity to all-time highs raise questions about whether execution can justify current valuations.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Oil Jumps 5%, S&P 500 Drops As Iran Strikes UAE Port
items.summary: U.S. stocks fell Monday as an Iranian drone strike on a UAE oil facility sent Brent crude above $114 a barrel, raising inflation concerns and expectations of a potential Fed rate hike by March 2027. The S&P 500 dropped 0.5%, the Dow fell 1.0%, and the Nasdaq 100 declined 0.7%. Energy stocks rallied while transportation, logistics, and rate-sensitive sectors suffered significant losses. Defense stocks gained on Pentagon spending narratives, while software and crypto-related equities found strength.

items.title: Here's Why GXO Logistics Shares Slumped (Hint: It's Amazon Related)
items.summary: Amazon's launch of Amazon Supply Chain Services (ASCS) caused GXO Logistics shares to drop nearly 13% as the company now offers freight, distribution, fulfillment, and parcel shipping to enterprise customers—a market segment GXO traditionally serves. While Amazon's move threatens GXO's business outlook, the company's more complex contract logistics offerings may be less impacted, and the announcement could raise awareness about logistics outsourcing opportunities.

items.title: Earnings Strength Moves Into Focus as the Busiest Week of Q1 Season Begins
items.summary: The S&P 500 is on track for its sixth consecutive quarter of double-digit earnings growth at 15.1%, driven by a 46% expansion in Information Technology. The Magnificent Seven tech companies reported strong revenue growth, but investor reactions were mixed based on capital expenditure guidance. With 63% of S&P 500 companies having reported, 84% beat EPS estimates and 81% beat revenue expectations. The busiest week of Q1 2026 earnings season begins with 3,213 companies set to report, including major names like Palantir, Disney, Uber, McDonald's, and AMD.

items.title: Why the iShares Semiconductor ETF Jumped 40% in April
items.summary: The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) surged 40% in April 2026, driven by strong semiconductor demand fueled by AI adoption. Key catalysts included impressive earnings reports from Intel, TSMC, and NXP, major cloud infrastructure deals for CoreWeave, and increased capital expenditure forecasts from hyperscalers like Meta. However, the sector now trades at a high valuation with a P/E ratio of 52.3.

items.title: Prediction: Amazon Stock Will Soon Join the $3 Trillion Club
items.summary: Amazon is positioned to become the fifth company to reach $3 trillion market cap, needing only a 4% gain from current levels. The company reported strong Q1 2026 results with 17% YoY sales growth, triple-digit AI revenue growth, and expansion across e-commerce, AWS, advertising, and its new satellite broadband service Amazon Leo.

items.title: 4 Reasons Alphabet's Cloud Growth Outpaced Its Larger Rivals
items.summary: Google Cloud achieved 63% revenue growth in Q1 2026, significantly outpacing Microsoft Azure (40%) and Amazon AWS (28%). The outperformance is driven by four key factors: strong adoption of Google Gemini AI models with 800% yearly growth in cloud gen AI revenue, development of custom Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) reducing Nvidia dependency, massive data advantages from Google Search and other services enabling superior AI model training, and a growing backlog of nearly $460 billion reflecting strong AI-native project demand.

items.title: Great News: Broadcom Just Strengthened Its AI Growth Story
items.summary: Broadcom has emerged as a hidden winner in AI infrastructure with improving revenue, margins, and contract visibility. However, the stock's significant rally has raised concerns about valuation, as the current price may already reflect substantial optimism about future growth.

items.title: Meta AI Capex (Up 7%) Is a Bad Surprise -- Could This Be the Next Metaverse?
items.summary: Meta Platforms reported higher-than-expected AI capital expenditure guidance for 2026 ($125-145 billion vs. prior $115-135 billion), causing its stock to drop 8.6%. Unlike competitors Alphabet and Amazon, Meta lacks a clear monetization strategy for its massive AI investments, raising concerns it may repeat its failed Metaverse experiment.

items.title: The Comeback Kid? Where Will Intel Be in 1 Year?
items.summary: Intel shows signs of recovery with strong Q1 2026 results, including 7.2% year-over-year revenue growth to $13.6 billion and improved gross margins. The company's strategic pivot toward semiconductor foundry services is gaining traction, with a major manufacturing deal signed with Amazon and ongoing talks with Google. Intel is also part of a $100 billion U.S. chip factory expansion plan, suggesting the company may have more competitive strength than previously thought.

items.title: Nvidia Rival Cerebras Reloads IPO Bid With $3.5 Billion Goal
items.summary: Cerebras Systems is pursuing an IPO to raise up to $3.5 billion, planning to offer 28 million shares priced between $115-$125 each on Nasdaq under ticker 'CBRS'. The company has received strong investor demand exceeding $10 billion and recently secured an $850 million credit facility. CEO Andrew Feldman claims Cerebras hardware enables AI models to run faster than NVIDIA's systems, positioning the company as a direct competitor in the AI chip sector.

items.title: Apple Just Gave Investors a $100 Billion Reason to Rethink the AI Spending Race
items.summary: Apple authorized an additional $100 billion share repurchase program while maintaining disciplined capital expenditures of only $4.3 billion in the first half of fiscal 2026. Unlike competitors investing hundreds of billions in AI infrastructure, Apple pursues a less capital-intensive edge-based AI strategy. The company reported strong fiscal Q2 results with 17% revenue growth, 22% EPS growth, and generated $82 billion in operating cash flow, returning $45 billion to shareholders through dividends and buybacks.

items.title: GameStop's Cohen Threatens Proxy War In High-Stakes eBay Bid
items.summary: GameStop has submitted an unsolicited $56 billion bid to acquire eBay at $125 per share (46% premium), with Ryan Cohen threatening a proxy fight if rejected. The proposal includes $2 billion in planned cost reductions and leveraging GameStop's 1,600 retail locations. eBay shares surged 9% to a new 52-week high in premarket trading, while GameStop fell 3%.

items.title: Danaher Corporation (DHR) Completes Merger Agreement with Masimo - GuruFocus
items.summary: Danaher Corporation (DHR) Completes Merger Agreement with Masimo    GuruFocus

items.title: This Is My Favorite AI Chip Stock (By Far) -- and It's Not Nvidia, Intel, or Broadcom
items.summary: Amazon's custom silicon chip business has reached a $20 billion annual revenue run rate with 40% sequential growth, positioning it as one of the top three data center chip businesses globally. CEO Andy Jassy stated the business could generate $50 billion annually if operated as a standalone company. With major customers like Anthropic and OpenAI committed to multi-gigawatt capacity and next-generation chips nearly fully subscribed, Amazon offers AI hardware exposure without pure-play chip valuations, though risks include elevated capital spending pressuring free cash flow.

items.title: Got $1,000? Here Are Three Incredible AI Stocks to Get Started
items.summary: The article recommends three AI stocks for beginner investors with $1,000: Nvidia, a semiconductor giant benefiting from massive AI computing demand with 73% YoY revenue growth; Alphabet, leveraging AI through Google Cloud (63% YoY growth) and Gemini integration; and Nebius, a high-growth neocloud company expecting 523% revenue growth in 2026, though with higher risk.

items.title: This Often Underappreciated Growth Stock Is Holding Its Own Against Giants Amazon and Alphabet. Time to Buy?
items.summary: Roku reported strong Q1 2026 results with 28% platform revenue growth and crossed 100 million streaming households globally. While profitability improved significantly and management raised full-year guidance, the stock's 60x P/E valuation leaves little margin for safety. The company faces intense competition from tech giants Amazon and Alphabet in the streaming and advertising space, and some business segments show weakness.

items.title: Why Meta Platforms Stock Plummeted This Week
items.summary: Meta Platforms stock fell 9.8% this week despite beating Q1 earnings and revenue expectations. The decline was driven by concerns over a sequential decline in daily active users (attributed to disruptions in Iran and WhatsApp restrictions in Russia) and Meta's announcement of increased capital expenditure targets for 2026, raising capex guidance to $125-145 billion from $115-135 billion to fund AI investments.

items.title: Alphabet Is Dropping $190 Billion on AI Infrastructure: 3 Semiconductor Stocks Set to Win Big
items.summary: Alphabet increased its capital expenditure guidance to $180-$190 billion with plans for even higher spending in 2027, signaling continued momentum in AI infrastructure investment. This spending surge is expected to benefit semiconductor companies Broadcom, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), and Nvidia, which supply chips and processors for AI data centers.

items.title: StellarSpace stellt bei einer Auftaktveranstaltung im Silicon Valley Produkte der neuen Generation für die industrielle Automatisierung vor
items.summary: StellarSpace, a global leader in industrial automation, launched its full-stack industrial intelligence product portfolio at the Global Embodied Intelligence AI Innovation Summit in Silicon Valley on April 28, 2026. The company introduced next-generation PLCs, servo systems, frequency converters, and industrial motors designed for AI-driven industrial applications. CEO Tan Cong highlighted the company's role in providing 'muscles and nerves' for embodied robots and noted international customer orders from multiple regions including the USA, Denmark, Italy, Turkey, UAE, Malaysia, Indonesia, and CIS countries.

items.title: Alphabet Is Outgrowing Amazon and Microsoft Where It Matters Most -- Why This Makes Alphabet Stock a Buy
items.summary: Alphabet's Google Cloud achieved 63% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026, significantly outpacing Microsoft Azure's 40% and Amazon AWS's 28% growth. With a nearly doubled backlog of $460 billion and expanding operating margins, Google Cloud is positioned as the fastest-growing cloud platform during the AI infrastructure buildout, though Alphabet's increased capital expenditure guidance to $180-190 billion introduces execution risk.
